flashcards.front: What is the primary purpose of virtualization in data center automation?
flashcards.back: To abstract physical hardware resources and enable software-defined management, provisioning, and orchestration of IT resources.

flashcards.front: Differentiate between Type 1 and Type 2 hypervisors.
flashcards.back: Type 1 (bare-metal) runs directly on hardware; Type 2 (hosted) runs on top of an operating system. Type 1 offers better performance for production environments.

flashcards.front: What is live migration and why is it important for automation?
flashcards.back: Live migration enables moving running VMs between physical hosts without downtime, facilitating maintenance, load balancing, and resource optimization.

flashcards.front: How does Infrastructure as Code (IaC) contribute to data center automation?
flashcards.back: IaC allows infrastructure to be defined and managed through code, enabling version control, reproducibility, and automated provisioning.

flashcards.front: What is virtual machine sprawl and how can it be mitigated?
flashcards.back: VM sprawl is uncontrolled proliferation of VMs. Mitigation strategies include lifecycle management policies, automated decommissioning, and resource monitoring.
